name: overlord-vscode
description: A live board of your Claude Code sessions in VS Code. One colored eye per session: red when it needs you, amber when working, green when just finished. Expandable cards with a live activity feed and hover preview, model + context telemetry, and configurable launch pills. Click the eye to jump to the exact terminal, see which session you're in, and have your terminal tab names restored after a window reload. Catches the questions and go-ahead requests that Agent View reports as idle.

overlord.claudePath.description: Command used to run the Claude Code CLI. Leave as 'claude' if it's on your PATH, or set the full path to the binary.

overlord.pollMs.description: How often (milliseconds) to poll `claude agents --json`. Each poll spawns the CLI (~0.5s), so lower values cost more CPU.

overlord.sound.description: Play a soft notification sound when a session needs you.

overlord.usage.description: Show your Claude usage limits (session/weekly/per-model) pinned at the top of the board. When on, Overlord reads your local Claude login token and makes one plain usage read (GET /api/oauth/usage — 0 tokens, not an AI call) each minute; nothing leaves your machine except that request to Anthropic's own API. Off by default.

overlord.doneFlashSeconds.description: How long a session that just finished (busy -> idle) shows a green 'done' flash before settling to grey 'idle'.

overlord.detectTypedQuestions.description: Claude Code reports 'idle' both when a session finishes and when it ends its turn on a typed question. When on, Overlord peeks the transcript's last message and shows 'needs you' if it's a question. Turn off to show only Claude Code's native status.

overlord.newSessionCommand.description: Command run in the terminal when you click '+ New session' (e.g. 'claude', 'claude --resume').

overlord.device.enabled.description: Opt-in. Serve the session grid to an Overlord hardware screen on your network (starts a local TCP server + discovery beacon). Leave off unless you have the companion screen.

overlord.device.port.description: TCP port the Overlord screen connects to.

overlord.defaultDetail.description: How session cards start: collapsed, expanded, or remembering the last state you set per session. Click a card's text to toggle.
enumDescriptions: Remember each card's last state (survives window reloads)

overlord.feedEvents.description: Maximum number of recent activity events shown on an expanded card.

overlord.launcher1.name.description: Launch Pill 1 - label and terminal tab name. Falls back to the command.

overlord.launcher1.icon.description: Launch Pill 1 - icon shown on the pill. Any other emoji can be typed directly in settings.json.

overlord.launcher1.cwd.description: Launch Pill 1 - directory the terminal starts in (~ supported). Empty = home dir.

overlord.launcher1.command.description: Launch Pill 1 - command typed into a new editor-area terminal (your default shell profile), e.g. 'claude' or 'yolo'. Empty disables this pill.

overlord.launcher1.autoLaunch.description: Launch Pill 1 - start this session automatically when VS Code opens.

overlord.launcher2.name.description: Launch Pill 2 - label and terminal tab name. Falls back to the command.

overlord.launcher2.icon.description: Launch Pill 2 - icon shown on the pill. Any other emoji can be typed directly in settings.json.

overlord.launcher2.cwd.description: Launch Pill 2 - directory the terminal starts in (~ supported). Empty = home dir.

overlord.launcher2.command.description: Launch Pill 2 - command typed into a new editor-area terminal (your default shell profile), e.g. 'claude' or 'yolo'. Empty disables this pill.

overlord.launcher2.autoLaunch.description: Launch Pill 2 - start this session automatically when VS Code opens.

overlord.launcher3.name.description: Launch Pill 3 - label and terminal tab name. Falls back to the command.

overlord.launcher3.icon.description: Launch Pill 3 - icon shown on the pill. Any other emoji can be typed directly in settings.json.

overlord.launcher3.cwd.description: Launch Pill 3 - directory the terminal starts in (~ supported). Empty = home dir.

overlord.launcher3.command.description: Launch Pill 3 - command typed into a new editor-area terminal (your default shell profile), e.g. 'claude' or 'yolo'. Empty disables this pill.

overlord.launcher3.autoLaunch.description: Launch Pill 3 - start this session automatically when VS Code opens.
